prompt:
A 3D printed rocket body tube lies on a workbench, its smooth cylindrical shell printed in PLA or PETG with a subtle layer-line texture along its length. One end of the tube has clean internal shoulders or slots designed to mate with a fincan, and the other end is open, ready to accept a nose cone or coupler. A few additional modular rocket parts—nose cone, coupler, and motor mount—sit nearby, making it clear that this is a structural body segment for a modular model rocket.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk maker space with soft, even lighting, a small FDM printer visible in the blurred background, and neatly organized tools and filaments on shelves. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable text on any parts, and a composition that centers the body tube and its joint features while the printer and surrounding workspace remain softly out of focus, emphasizing precision-printed modular rocketry.
